titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Sangallensis 51
VL48
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Mt Mc Lc Jo

decoDesc.p: Black ink with rubrication. Highly decorated full-page colour pictures.

surrogates.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers pages 208-265. There are several instances of the final words of a line being written in blank space at the end of a line above or below, preceded by ///. Corrections are sometimes indicated by a very faint scratch.The transcription includes foliation, lineation, all punctuation and abbreviations. The æ digraph has also been recorded. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Change to verse division of 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: J-VL-048-B4K0V0
J-VL-048-B4K1V1
The correction is an 'l ' in the left margin
The end of the following line is written in the blank space remaining at the end of this one.
The end of the following line is written in the blank space remaining at the end of this one.
Hole in parchment.
The end of the following line is written in the blank space remaining at the end of this one.
The end of the following line is written in the blank space remaining at the end of this one.
The end of the next line is written in blank space here.
The end of the next line is written here in blank space.
sic
The next sentence is written one line down, with a sign indicating the omission.
The first eight words on this line are the missing words from the end of verse 6.
Hole in parchment.
Hole in parchment.
Hole in parchment.
Hole in parchment.
Hole in parchment.
Hole in parchment.
The word 'ouile' from the end of the next line appears in blank space at the end of this line.
Ornate initial 'A' with bird's head.
The correction is added in the line following the words 'fur est et latro' by the first hand.
Damage to parchment or erasure at beginning of line.
Hole in parchment.
Hole in parchment.
The letters 'r(un)t' from the end of the next line are written here in blank space.
The word 'p(ro)ximu¬' is written in the right margin by a later hand.
Cross in left margin.
The words at the end of the next line are written here in blank space.
This is not the abbreviation used elsewhere for quae, but it is difficult to see what else could be intended.
Large hole in parchment.
There are ink marks over 'enim', but it is unclear whether these are accidental or an illegible correction.
The word 'illa' has a scratch through it.
The letters 're' have a scratch through them.
Hole in parchment.
The word 'uere' has a scratch through it.
The words 'trans torrentem' are written in blank space at the end of this line.
The letters 'ul' of 'faculis' seem to have a scratch through them, but no other correction has been supplied.
The next two lines are written in a slightly smaller size of script.
At this point, the first hand gives up and the last two lines of text are written in a later hand.
The subscription is in a third, even later hand.
J-VL-048-B4K22V0b
